title: Gated RNN Unit Architecture Comparison
description: A side-by-side visual breakdown of the internal gating mechanisms within a standard RNN, LSTM, and GRU cell, highlighting the flow of information and the function of each gate.
relationships: Input and previous hidden state are concatenated and fed into all gates
Gates control the flow of information via sigmoid-activated multiplicative operations
The forget gate decides what information to discard from the cell state
The input gate and candidate state work together to update the cell state
The output gate filters the updated cell state to produce the new hidden state
GRU combines the forget and input gates into a single update gate and uses a reset gate to modulate the influence of the previous hidden state
